ProjectDescription: The digital employment platform connects job seekers with disabilities who lack formal certifications with inclusive job opportunities. Through a web application, users create structured profiles describing their abilities, preferences, limitations, and preferred work environments, focusing on practical capabilities rather than formal credentials. Employers use the Employer Job Posting Interface to register their organizations and define roles adapted for candidates with disabilities, including workplace conditions and accessibility requirements. An AI-based matching service, part of the AI Matching Module, analyzes both profiles and job characteristics to recommend suitable opportunities. The platform is operated through a nonprofit middle tier with the Reporting and Analytics Module for monitoring activity and outcomes, and integrates the Accessibility API Module to enable voice-based user input.

Lists.Name: Sprint 8
Lists.Description:
This is the final stabilization push. Your mission is to switch from feature building to a collaborative "Bug Hunting" phase alongside the entire team to ensure the platform is production-ready. 
 
 You are moving from the "feature" phase to the "quality" phase. This sprint is entirely focused on identifying glitches and ensuring the user experience is flawless before the final handoff. Since everyone is doing QA, you will monitor the Meeting Room for every "Bugs case" logged and address them in your dedicated branch. 
 
 Success means a pixel-perfect, error-free frontend that has been fully merged into the main repository.

Cards.Name: Infrastructure Audit & High-Level Data Modeling
Cards.Description:
Welcome to the team! 
 
 This week is your "Technical Landing": a low-pressure space to explore the project's foundations. 
 
 Your objective is to partner with the PM to translate their business journey into a technical blueprint. 
 
 You will verify the environment and create the "Big Picture" database model that will serve as our project's technical map.
ChecklistItems: Team Kickoff: Participate in the "Project Kickoff" to meet your teammates and understand the project’s high-level mission.
Local Repository Setup: Clone the provided GitHub repository to your local developer environment to begin your technical exploration.
Sprint Branch Initialization (Mentor Panel): Use the mentor buttons panel to create your official branch for the current sprint.
Local Sync Verification (Local Environment): From your local environment, push a "Health Check" commit to your branch to confirm your local IDE is perfectly synced with the repository.

GitHub Cycle Practice (Mentor Panel): Return to the mentor buttons panel to create a Pull Request (PR) and merge your branch into main. Use this to gain a hands-on understanding of the project's GitHub cycle.
Infrastructure Audit: Connect to the Postgres DB and perform a "Stress Test" (e.g., creating and deleting a dummy table) to ensure the environment is stable for future data.

Collaborative Journey & Persona Review: Sit down with the PM to review their Job Seeker and Employer Journey Diagram and review the UI/UX Designer's new User Personas. Use this session to brainstorm the core entities and relationships (e.g., Job Seekers, Employers, Job Opportunities, Matches) needed to support the entire system.
High-Level Schema Diagram: Based on your review with the PM, create a visual ERD (Entity Relationship Diagram) showing the high-level data architecture.
Blueprint Centralization: Save your schema diagram in the Meeting Room so the team can reference our "Data Source of Truth." (Your Mentor can guide you on the process).

Cards.Name: Frontend Orientation & Environmental Foundation
Cards.Description:
Welcome to the front line! 
 
 We are thrilled to have you on board. 
 
 This first week is your "Technical Landing" - a low-pressure space to explore your new digital playground. 
 
 While the PM and UI/UX teams define the project's soul, your objective is to ensure your development environment is perfectly tuned and to collaborate on the project's global structure. 
 
 You are the team's bridge between design and code.
ChecklistItems: [ ] Team Kickoff: Participate in the "Sprint 1 Kickoff" to meet the team and align on our high-level mission.
[ ] Local Repository Setup: Clone the provided GitHub repository to your local developer environment to begin your technical setup.
[ ] Sprint Branch Initialization (Mentor Panel): Use the mentor buttons panel to create your official branch for the current sprint.

[ ] Local Sync Verification (Local Environment): From your local environment, push a "Health Check" commit to your branch to confirm your local IDE and build tools are perfectly synced with the repository.

[ ] GitHub Cycle Practice (Mentor Panel): Return to the mentor buttons panel to create a Pull Request (PR) and merge your branch into main. Use this to gain a hands-on understanding of the project's GitHub cycle.
[ ] Tech Stack Audit: Explore the project's frontend boilerplate (React/Next.js/etc.) and verify that all necessary dependencies and linting rules are correctly installed.
[ ] Collaborative Journey Review: Sit down with the PM and the Backend Developer to review the Global Journey Diagram. Use this to identify potential UI complexities or navigation challenges early.

[ ] Global Styling Foundation: Based on the UI/UX Designer's initial research, begin initializing the project's "Foundations"—set up the global CSS variables for the color palette, typography scales, and spacing units.

Cards.Name: Ecosystem & CRM Foundation
Cards.Description:
Welcome to the team!

We are so excited to have you as our "Market Intelligence" lead.

This first week is a low-pressure opportunity for you to learn about the employment ecosystem we are entering.

Your mission is to map out the "playing field" - the landscape of inclusive employment and disability workforce integration - and identify who the key players are.

Think of the AI Mentor as your research consultant who will hold your hand while you learn our internal CRM module.

This week is all about discovery and building a solid knowledge base for the team.
ChecklistItems: Team Kickoff: Join the "Sprint 1 Kickoff" to meet the team and understand our shared starting point.
Anchor Alignment: Start an introductory conversation with the AI Customer to understand the business world they operate in every day.
CRM & Research Setup: Ask the AI Mentor for a step-by-step walkthrough of the CRM module and for professional guidance on creating a "Stakeholder Map."

Industry Discovery: Conduct "Desk Research" on the inclusive employment ecosystem to understand how it works (e.g., how job seekers with disabilities connect with employers and find suitable work opportunities).
Competitive Analysis: Review 2-3 existing global solutions and summarize how they approach this industry.
Stakeholder Mapping: Identify the key groups involved (job seekers with disabilities, employers, and nonprofit operators) and input these entities into your CRM screen.
NSM Prep: Get familiar with the term NSM (Next Sprint Meeting); you’ll share your ecosystem findings here to help the team stay grounded in the industry's reality.

Cards.Name: Vision, Discovery & The PRD Foundation
Cards.Description:
Welcome to the helm! 
 
 Your mission this week is to transform initial ambiguity into a solid strategic roadmap. 
 
 You are the Strategic Detective, digging deep into the "Why" and "Who" of this project. 
 
 The Big Picture: While we are establishing the foundations now, keep in mind that the actual construction of our first module begins in Sprint 3. 
 
 This week is about building the trust and the blueprint: the Project PRD - required to make that build successful.
ChecklistItems: Project Kickoff: Lead the official "Project Kickoff" in the meeting room. Set the vision, align the team on the mission, and facilitate introductions.
Kickoff Synthesis: Publish a concise meeting summary and a list of immediate action items in the Team Chat to ensure Day 1 alignment.
Relationship Building: Initiate introductory conversations with each teammate (BE, FE, UI/UX, BizDev) to establish a personal connection and a supportive team culture.
Team Connection Recap: Post a high-level summary of the team’s collective strengths and support needs in the Team Chat to reinforce the collaborative foundation.
PRD Initialization: Initialize the Product Requirements Document (PRD). This is the central home for the project’s DNA. Use it to document every strategic decision you make this week.

Problem Validation: Interview the AI Customer to confirm reality vs. assumptions regarding their current workflow. Document their "Pain Points" and "Success Criteria" in the Discovery section of your PRD.
Customer Insight Broadcast: Share the key takeaways and prioritized action items from the customer interview in the Team Chat to ground the team in the user's reality.
North Star & KPIs: Define the project's North Star Metric and 3–5 supporting KPIs. Add these to the PRD to serve as the decision filter for the entire journey.
Actor Alignment: Identify the main actors (e.g., Job Seekers, Employers, Nonprofit Operators). Map out their primary responsibilities and "Needs" within the User Persona section of the PRD.
Journey Mapping & Centralization: Create a visual diagram of the end-to-end job seeking journey. Integrate this diagram directly into the PRD so it serves as the project’s visual anchor.
PRD Accessibility: Save the PRD in the Squad Room. Ensure it is available to everyone and clearly marked as the project's "Source of Truth."
Team Journey Demonstration: Set a meeting with the entire team to demonstrate the Journey Map and walk through the Draft PRD. Gather feedback and ensure everyone sees the "Big Picture."
Vision Alignment Summary: Publish the final meeting summary and a consolidated list of feedback-driven action items in the Team Chat to officially close the loop on Sprint 1.

Cards.Name: Ecosystem Discovery & Research Foundation
Cards.Description:
Welcome to the team! 
 
 As our lead designer, you are the architect of the user's experience. 
 
 This first week is your "Discovery" phase: a low-pressure space to understand the industry and define who we are building for. 
 
 Before diving into the visuals, you will coordinate your research roadmap with the Product Manager. 
 
 Your goal is to gather the competitive intelligence and persona insights needed to shape a high-impact design strategy in the weeks ahead.
ChecklistItems: Project Kickoff: Participate in the Project Kickoff meeting to align on the mission and meet your new teammates.
Product Manager Alignment: Meet with the Product Manager to plan your specific research tasks and milestones for the remainder of the sprint.
Figma Setup: Initialize your Figma project and link it to the Meeting Room. Ask the AI Mentor if you need instructions on how to sync the files.

Market Research: Analyze existing solutions in related fields (e.g., disability employment platforms, inclusive hiring tools, or workforce matching systems). Note the pros and cons of each and upload a summary to the Meeting Room.
Visual Inspiration Board: Create a Mood Board or "Inspiration Table" in Figma. Focus on UI patterns, color psychology, and layouts that align with the project goals.

User Personas: Define initial User Personas (e.g., job seeker, employer, nonprofit coordinator). Document their specific needs, motivations, and potential pain points. Add persona designs to the project's Figma.
User Journey Insights: Review the Global User Journey created by the Product Manager. Flag any specific areas or flows that require extra UX attention.

Cards.Name: Data Architecture & Technical Readiness
Cards.Description:
This week is about definition over execution.

Your goal is to move from the abstract "Big Picture" to a precise technical blueprint for the Job Seeker Profile form.

You are the architect of the data flow.

By the end of this week, you will have established the plan for how the Frontend and Backend communicate.

You aren't building the final logic yet; you are preparing the "manual" so that the team can build with total confidence in Sprint 3.
ChecklistItems: Environment Launch: Launch the sprint and create your Sprint 2 branch via the mentor buttons panel to begin your technical exploration.

Tooling and Library Audit: Review your current backend stack and libraries (e.g., validation libraries, database drivers). Ensure everything is optimized and ready for the specific data-entry and form-handling tasks coming next week.

Frontend Data Sync (General Handshake): Meet with the Frontend Developer to agree on the general "Blueprint" for how data will move between the UI and the server (e.g., Auth patterns, error handling, and endpoint structure).

Requirements Sync (Tables and Fields): Once the PM has finalized the User Story, meet to identify the specific Tables and Fields required to support the job seeker profile form. Map out exactly what data points need to be stored (e.g., abilities, work preferences, limitations, and preferred work environments).

Database Schema Blueprint: Update the Database Schema diagram based on the PM's finalized fields. This acts as the "Contract" for the project's data structure and serves as the team's single source of truth.

Mock Data Provision: Generate a set of "dummy" job seeker profile data. The goal is to give the Frontend Developer realistic data to work with in Sprint 3 before you have built the actual database logic.
Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to present your technical blueprint. Confirm that your architecture can support the validation rules required by the customer.

Blueprint Delivery (PR and Merge): Commit your final schema designs and mock data files to your branch. Create a Pull Request (PR) and merge it into main via the mentor buttons panel to lock in the technical blueprint for the build.

Cards.Name: UI Architecture & Component Scaffolding
Cards.Description:
This week, you are the "Architect" of the interface.

Before we start coding the actual logic for the Job Seeker Profile form in Sprint 3, you will build the reusable "Atoms" that make development fast and consistent.

You are building the "lego pieces" and the "hallways" of the application.

By the end of the week, the team should be able to click through the basic navigation of the flow, even if the forms don't "save" data yet.

This is where we build the skeleton so that next week, we can add the muscle.
ChecklistItems: Environment Launch: Launch the sprint and create your Sprint 2 branch via the mentor buttons panel to begin your technical exploration.
Component Library Initialization: Set up a dedicated workspace for your reusable UI components (e.g., a /components library folder or a Storybook setup).

Atomic Component Build: Build the foundational, "logic-less" components required for the job seeker profile form: Buttons, Input Fields (Text/Select), Loaders, and Success Modals. Apply the Visual Language (Colors and Typography) established by the Designer this week.

Frontend Data Sync: Meet with the Backend Developer to agree on the "Data Blueprint." Confirm the specific "Request" (what the form sends) and "Response" (what the server says back) so that your components are ready for real data.

App Navigation "Hallways": Set up the empty page containers and the URL paths for the Job Seeker Profile flow (e.g., /profile/create for the form and /profile/success for the confirmation). This ensures the structural journey is ready for the logic in Sprint 3.
UI/UX Handshake: Review the Designer’s final design. Ensure general synchronization regarding technical feasibility and confirm that the layout aligns with the UI components you are preparing.
Milestone: Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to present your component library and technical readiness. Show the team the "UI Atoms" and the clickable "Hallways" you’ve built.

UI Delivery (PR & Merge): Commit your foundational components and navigation routes to your branch. Create a Pull Request (PR) and merge it into main via the mentor buttons panel. This locks in the UI architecture before the build phase begins.

Cards.Name: Job Seeker Profile Development
Cards.Description:
This week, you serve as the project’s Strategic Pulse.

Your mission is to maintain a dual-stream focus: Internal Alignment to deeply understand the module’s technical evolution, and External Validation to ground that progress in user reality.

You are responsible for ensuring that the Job Seeker Profile form is not just a technical achievement, but a user-ready solution.

You will bridge the gap between the development team’s potential constraints and the actual operational habits of job seekers with disabilities.
ChecklistItems:
User Sync: At the start of the week, consult with the AI Customer to identify "unknowns" regarding the job seeker’s profile data.
Focus: Clarify operational details that will affect the build (e.g., "Does the job seeker usually know their exact abilities and work preferences, or do they think in terms of 'types of tasks' or 'preferred work environments'?").

Internal Product Alignment: Once the PM and UI/UX Designer have drafted the initial User Story and design, meet to conduct a deep-dive. 
 Goal: Ensure you can articulate exactly how the profile creation logic and validations are intended to work before you head into user testing.

Field Stakeholder Outreach: Conduct targeted interviews with 2-3 potential job seekers (e.g., individuals with disabilities seeking employment). 
 Validation: Present the high-level concept of the profile creation module to gather raw, unbiased feedback.

Operational Habit Validation: Analyze whether the proposed digital workflow (specifically the guided questions and input fields) aligns with the "on-the-ground" behavior of job seekers wuth disabilities during profile creation.

CRM Intelligence Logging: Use the CRM button in the Meeting Room to document all field insights and stakeholder profiles. 
 Data Management: Ensure you are managing the prospect list (entering/editing) so the entire team has a "Data Source of Truth" regarding user needs.

Strategic Resource Management: Use the Resources button to upload and link relevant market research, competitive analysis, or user experience examples. 
 Benchmark: Ensure the team has access to these references for inspiration.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Present your "Field Intelligence" at the NSM. 
 Prioritization: Help the team prioritize profile fields and validation logic based on real-world urgency versus technical "nice-to-haves."
Sprint 4 Staggered Look-Ahead: Work with the PM to identify the key stakeholders needed for the Employer Job Posting Interface validation, which will be your primary focus next week.

Cards.Name: Job Seeker Profile Development
Cards.Description:
This week, you are the Bridge between the high-level journey and the actual build. 
 
 Your mission is to translate the vision from Sprint 1 into a detailed, actionable User Story for the Job Seeker Profile Module. 
 
 Sprint 2 is about removing every technical and logical blocker before we start coding in Sprint 3. 
 
 You are the team’s Unlocker. 
 
 We are focusing entirely on the "Profile Foundation" - capturing the needs of diverse job seeker profiles with disabilities, including their abilities, preferences, and limitations. 
 
 Success means a job seeker can seamlessly enter their details, articulate their strengths, and manage their employment profile via a mobile-optimized form. 
 
 By the end of this week, the team must know exactly what to build, why, and how. 
 
 Every decision you document now regarding the "Seeker Experience" ensures the developers and designers can execute the build with total focus and zero ambiguity.
ChecklistItems: Meeting Orchestration: Schedule a short Sprint 2 Status Meeting at the start of the week for team alignment. Schedule the Next Sprint Meeting (NSM) for the second half of the week.
Status Sync Recap: Publish a concise summary of the week’s setup tasks and any immediate blockers identified during the status meeting to the Team Chat.

User Validation: Interview the AI Customer to finalize the required profile fields (limit to max 6) and identify the specific IF/THEN validation rules, such as rules for specific work environments or ability descriptions.

The User Story Deep-Dive: Locate the existing Job Seeker Profile card in the "User Stories" Trello list and fill it out completely: 
 (1) Description Field: Write the formal story ("As a... I want... so that...") , define the In-Scope/Out-of-Scope lists, and include a Data Table listing every Field Name, Field Type (Text, Number, etc.), and whether it is Required (Yes/No). 
 (2) Acceptance Criteria Checklist: Populate the pre-made, empty checklist on the card with specific "Done" conditions, for example, "Profile updated successfully" or "Mandatory field errors trigger correctly.","

Design Alignment: Sync with the UI/UX Designer to review the Profile Form Design and success screens. Ensure the final design, including the visual language, is saved in the Squad Room before the sprint ends.

Technical Requirements Sync: Meet with the Backend Developer once your User Story is drafted. Help them identify the specific Tables and Fields needed to support the profile logic based on your Data Specification.
Technical Readiness Audit: Verify the development environment. Confirm the BE has the Schema ready and has generated Mock Data, and that the FE has built the UI Atoms and Navigation Hallways.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Lead the planning session. 
 Agenda: 
 (1) Present the User Story, Data Specs, and AC 
 (2) Facilitate T-shirt sizing (S/M/L) 
 (3) Confirm developer understanding 
 (4) Assign owners
Sprint 3 Launch Sync: Publish a consolidated summary of the build goals and a clear list of prioritized action items in the Team Chat to formally trigger the start of the execution phase.

Cards.Name: Profile Visualization & Interactive Prototyping
Cards.Description:
This week, you translate research into reality.

Your mission is to move from the mood boards and personas of Sprint 1 directly into a high-fidelity Visual Language that establishes trust and clarity.

You are the Guardian of the Brand. By the end of this week, the developers should have a "Design Source of Truth" including the final color palette, typography, and polished UI layouts for the Job Seeker Profile form.

Your goal is to ensure the interface doesn't just work, it feels professional, reliable, and accessible from the very first screen.
ChecklistItems: Logo & Visual Language: Interview the AI Customer regarding the product name, logo guidelines, and overall visual direction. Design the product logo and establish the visual language in Figma (e.g., Color Palette and Typography).
User Requirement Sync: Interview the AI Customer to identify specific user needs for the job seeker profile and verify any functional expectations.

PM Requirement Sync: Meet with the PM as soon as the job seeker profile user story is drafted. Review the required data fields and validation logic to ensure your design supports all business and technical constraints.
Profile Form Design: Create a mobile-first design of the job seeker profile form in Figma. Focus on layout hierarchy and ease of use on smaller screens to ensure a smooth user experience.

States & Notifications: Design success and error states for all form fields and add them to the Figma design system. Create the corresponding success and error notifications for the post-submission flow.

Technical Handshake: Sync with the Frontend Developer to review your designs. Ensure general synchronization regarding technical feasibility and confirm that your UI components align with the project's component library.
Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to present the final design to the team. This will serve as the "North Star" for the upcoming development in Sprint 3.
Sprint 4 Look-Ahead: Briefly coordinate with the Product Manager to identify the core requirements for the Employer Job Posting Interface. This will be your research focus starting next week.

Cards.Name: Feature Logic & API Execution
Cards.Description:
This is it. The Build Week. 
 
 You are no longer just an architect. You are the Profile Specialist. 
 
 Your mission is to build the framework that allows a job seeker to create and manage their employment profile successfully. 
 
 By the end of this sprint, when a user completes the guided questions on the frontend, your backend must capture that data, verify it against the business rules, and save it permanently to the database. 
 
 You are responsible for ensuring that the data "Profile Foundation" is secure, accurate, and ready for the next stage of the project.
ChecklistItems: Sprint 3 Launch: Participate in the Sprint Meeting led by the PM. Review the User Story and Acceptance Criteria to ensure you are 100% aligned on the build goals.
Environment Setup: Create your Sprint 3 branch via the mentor buttons panel to begin the build.
Database Migration: Implement the physical tables in the database based on the schema you finalized in Sprint 2. Ensure all fields (IDs, timestamps, and data types) are correctly configured.
Validation Engine Build: Code the IF/THEN logic as defined in the PM's User Story (e.g., ensuring a preference isn't left blank or a limitation is accurately described).

API Endpoint Development: Build the POST endpoint that will receive the profile data. Ensure the response body includes all necessary parameters for UI responsiveness (e.g., specific error codes, unique record IDs, or updated state objects).
Unit Testing: Write and execute unit tests for your validation logic and database service. Ensure that "bad data" is rejected with the correct error codes and "good data" is prepared for storage.
Peer Code Review: Share your code with the AI Mentor for a review session to ensure your logic is clean, secure, and follows best practices.

Integration Test (Internal): Use your "Mock Data" from last week to test the endpoint. Confirm that valid profile data is saved and invalid data triggers the correct error response codes before involving the frontend.

Frontend Handshake: Pair with the Frontend Developer to "wire up" the profile form. Verify that their UI components are successfully sending the data to your endpoint and receiving the expected response parameters.

Next Module Requirements Sync: Meet with the PM once the Employer Job Posting User Story is drafted to identify the specific tables and fields needed to support the upcoming logic based on the new Data Specification.
Technical Readiness Audit: Prepare the environment for the PM's audit by ensuring the Employer Job Posting schema is ready and comprehensive mock data has been generated for the next phase.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to plan the development of the Employer Job Posting Interface. This session serves as the primary planning phase for the upcoming sprint, though it can also be used to demonstrate that the profile data is being successfully captured and stored.
Feature Shipping (PR & Merge): Push your final code, create a Pull Request (PR), and merge it into main via the mentor buttons panel to officially complete the Profile Foundation.

Cards.Name: Job Seeker Profile Logic & API Integration
Cards.Description:
This is the Build Week. 
 
 You are no longer just building components. You are building the Experience. 
 
 Your mission is to take the "Atoms" from Sprint 2 and wire them into a fully functional, responsive profile form that captures user input and server feedback. 
 
 By the end of this sprint, a user should be able to fill out the profile form, see real-time validation, and receive a clear success or error message from the server. 
 
 You are responsible for the "Handshake" between the user and the data engine.
ChecklistItems: Sprint 3 Launch: Participate in the Sprint Meeting led by the PM. Review the User Story and Acceptance Criteria to ensure the UI behavior matches the business requirements.
Environment Setup: Create your Sprint 3 branch via the mentor buttons panel to begin the build.

Temporary Access Point: Add a "Job Seeker Profile Form" button to the index.html file "frontend-buttons" panel. This ensures the form is accessible to all team members via the "Live Project" link during development. Note that a professional Landing Page will be properly designed toward the end of the project (Sprint 7).
Form Logic Implementation: Transform your static components into a dynamic profile form. Implement the local state management to capture user input for all fields defined in the PM's Data Table.
Client-Side Validation: Code the immediate UI feedback logic (e.g., preventing empty submissions, checking input formats, or ensuring correct data types) before the data even hits the server.
API Integration (The Wiring): Connect your profile form to the Backend's POST endpoint. Implement the fetch or axios calls to send the user data to the server.

Responsive Feedback & Error Handling: Use the server's response parameters to trigger UI changes. Build the logic for loading states, specific field error messages, and the redirect to the Success "Hallway."
Peer Code Review: Share your code with the AI Mentor for a review session to ensure your state management is efficient and your UI logic is robust.
Integration Test (Internal): Run a full end-to-end test of the flow. Confirm that the profile form correctly handles both a "Happy Path" (success) and "Edge Cases" (validation errors).
Backend Handshake: Pair with the Backend Developer to verify that your "Request" body perfectly matches their endpoint's expectations and that you are correctly parsing their "Response" parameters.

Next Module Requirements Sync: Meet with the PM once the Employer Job Posting User Story is drafted to identify any unique UI requirements, table structures, or navigation hallways needed for the next phase.
Technical Readiness Audit: Prepare the environment for the PM's audit by ensuring the Employer Job Posting UI atoms and navigation hallways are built and ready for the Sprint 4 integration.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to plan the development of the Employer Job Posting Interface. This session serves as the primary planning phase for the upcoming sprint, though it can also be used to demonstrate the live input flow and successful navigation to the confirmation screen.
Feature Shipping (PR & Merge): Push your final code, create a Pull Request (PR), and merge it into main via the mentor buttons panel to officially complete the Profile Experience.

Cards.Name: Employer Job Posting Strategy and Validation
Cards.Description:
This week, you serve as the project’s Employer Job Posting Strategist. 
 
 Your mission is to maintain a dual-stream focus: Internal Alignment to deeply understand the next phase of the job posting process, and External Validation to ground the upcoming Employer Job Posting Interface in market reality. 
 
 You are responsible for ensuring that the job posting experience is not just a technical interface, but a tool that fits the operational needs of employers seeking to attract candidates with disabilities. 
 
 You will bridge the gap between the development team’s potential constraints and the actual hiring practices of employers, HR staff, and recruitment coordinators.
ChecklistItems: mployer Sync: At the start of the week, consult with the AI Customer to identify "unknowns" regarding the Employer Job Posting Interface for jobs for people with disabilities. Clarify posting details that will affect the build, such as how employers prefer to input job tasks, accessibility considerations, and required skills.

Internal Product Alignment: Once the PM and UI/UX Designer have drafted the initial User Story and design for the Employer Job Posting Interface, meet to conduct a deep-dive. Ensure you can articulate exactly how the job posting logic and status transitions are intended to work.

Field Stakeholder Outreach: Conduct targeted interviews with 2-3 potential "Employers" or "Recruiters" (e.g., HR managers, business owners, or recruitment leads) who hire or support people with disabilities. Present the high-level concept of the job posting interface to gather raw, unbiased feedback.

Operational Habit Validation: Analyze whether the proposed digital job posting workflow (specifically the input fields and editing capabilities) aligns with the "on-the-ground" behavior of an employer managing job listings.

CRM Intelligence Logging: Use the CRM button in the Meeting Room to document all job posting-related insights and stakeholder profiles. Ensure you are managing the prospect list so the team has a "Data Source of Truth" regarding the needs of employers.

Strategic Resource Management: Use the Resources button to upload and link relevant market research regarding job posting best practices or recruitment software. Ensure the team has access to these benchmarks for inspiration.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Present your "Field Intelligence" at the NSM. Help the team prioritize job posting features and business logic based on real-world operational urgency versus technical "nice-to-haves."
Sprint 5 Staggered Look-Ahead: Work with the PM to identify the key stakeholders needed for the validation of the AI Matching Agent, which will be your primary focus next week.

Cards.Name: Tactical Blueprint and User Story
Cards.Description:
This week, you are the Bridge between the current build and the next phase of the project. 
 
 Your mission is to translate the vision from the global roadmap into a detailed, actionable User Story for the Employer Job Posting Interface. 
 
 Sprint 3 is about removing every technical and logical blocker before we start coding in Sprint 4. 
 
 You are the team’s Unlocker. 
 
 We are shifting focus from job posting creation to management, exploring how employers will interact with, edit, and oversee the job listings within the system. 
 
 Success means defining a clear, actionable workflow that transforms job details into accessible, published listings. 
 
 By the end of this week, the team must know exactly what to build for the next module, why, and how. 
 
 Every decision you document now regarding the Job Posting Experience ensures the developers and designers can execute the build with total focus and zero ambiguity.
ChecklistItems: Meeting Orchestration: Schedule a short Sprint 3 Status Meeting at the start of the week for team alignment. Schedule the Next Sprint Meeting (NSM) for the second half of the week.
Status Sync Recap: Publish a concise summary of the week’s setup tasks and any immediate blockers identified during the status meeting to the Team Chat.

Customer Validation: Interview the AI Customer to finalize the required job posting views (e.g., Job Summary vs. Full Job Description) for jobs for people with disabilities and identify the specific business logic for the Edit/Update flow.

The User Story Deep-Dive: Locate the existing Employer Job Posting Interface card in the “User Stories” Trello list and fill it out completely:
(1) Description Field: Write the formal story ("As an employer... I want... so that..."), define the In-Scope/Out-of-Scope lists, and include a Data Table listing every Field Name (e.g., Job Title, Responsibilities, Skills, Environmental Conditions), Data Type, and required User Actions (e.g., Publish, Edit).
(2) Acceptance Criteria Checklist: Populate the pre-made, empty checklist on the card with specific "Done" conditions, such as validated job posting visibility, successful editing/updating of job details, and clear specification of the physical or cognitive abilities required for the role.

Design Alignment: Sync with the UI/UX Designer to review the interface designs and detail views. Ensure the final design, including the visual language, is saved in the Squad Room before the sprint ends.

Technical Requirements Sync: Meet with the Backend Developer once your User Story is drafted. Help them identify the specific Tables and Fields needed to support the Employer Job Posting logic based on your Data Specification.

Technical Readiness Audit: Verify the development environment. Confirm the BE has the Schema ready and has generated Mock Data, and that the FE has built the UI Atoms and Navigation Hallways for the job posting interface.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Lead the planning session. 
 Agenda: 
 (1) Present the User Story, Data Specs, and AC 
 (2) Facilitate T-shirt sizing (S/M/L)

Cards.Name: Job Posting Logic and Data Management
Cards.Description:
This week, you transition from the entry point to the job posting interface.

You are no longer just building forms for input. You are the Job Posting Specialist.

Your mission is to build the management systems and logic that allow employers to create, edit, and oversee job postings specifically designed for candidates with disabilities.

By the end of this sprint, your interface must be able to serve detailed job listings, including both descriptions and the physical or cognitive abilities required, to the users and execute updates based on employer actions.

You are responsible for ensuring that the Employer Job Posting Interface is robust, user-friendly, and ready to support the inclusive job listing workflow.

Environment Setup: Create your Sprint 4 branch via the mentor buttons panel to begin the build.
Database Migration/Updates: Implement any structural changes or new fields required for job posting management. Ensure indexes are correctly set for efficient searching and filtering.
Form Logic Build: Code the logic to handle job details input, ensuring that all fields for responsibilities, required skills, and environmental conditions are properly structured.

API Endpoint Development: Build the POST and GET endpoints for job listings, alongside the PATCH/PUT endpoints to handle updates. Ensure the response body includes the updated job listing objects for UI responsiveness.
Unit Testing: Write and execute unit tests for your job posting logic and update functions. Ensure that unauthorized or invalid submissions are rejected with the correct error codes.
Peer Code Review: Share your code with the AI Mentor for a review session to ensure your logic is clean, secure, and follows best practices.

Integration Test (Internal): Use your "Mock Data" from Sprint 3 to verify the job posting endpoints. Confirm that the job listings return the correct data and that updates persist in the database before involving the frontend.

Cards.Name: Strategic Pulse and Compatibility Validation
Cards.Description:
This week, you serve as the project’s Strategic Pulse.

Your mission is to maintain a dual-stream focus: Internal Alignment to deeply understand the AI Matching Module's technical evolution, and External Validation to ground the upcoming AI Matching Module in market reality.

You are responsible for ensuring that the matching experience is not just a technical algorithm, but a functional tool that fits the real-time employment needs of job seekers with disabilities and inclusive-minded employers.

You will bridge the gap between the development team’s potential constraints and the actual hiring practices and accessibility considerations for candidates with disabilities.
ChecklistItems: Customer Sync: At the start of the week, consult with the AI Customer to identify "unknowns" regarding the AI Matching Module. Clarify compatibility details that will affect the build, such as how job seekers with disabilities prioritize opportunities and what data they need while searching for roles that match their abilities and work preferences.

Internal Product Alignment: Once the PM and UI/UX Designer have drafted the initial User Story and design for the AI Matching Module, meet to conduct a deep-dive. Ensure you can articulate exactly how the matching logic and user interactions are intended to work.

Field Stakeholder Outreach: Conduct targeted interviews with 2–3 potential "Employers" or "Job Seekers" (e.g., hiring managers, recruiters, or job applicants) who work with or are candidates with disabilities. Present the high-level concept of the matching interface to gather raw, unbiased feedback.

Operational Habit Validation: Analyze whether the proposed digital matching workflow aligns with the "on-the-ground" behavior of a job seeker with disabilities managing their application process during a busy job search.

CRM Intelligence Logging: Use the CRM button in the Meeting Room to document all matching-related insights and stakeholder profiles. Ensure you are managing the prospect list so the team has a "Data Source of Truth" regarding the needs of the job seekers and employers.

Strategic Resource Management: Use the Resources button to upload and link relevant market research regarding job matching algorithms, user preferences, or employment benchmarks. Ensure the team has access to these references for inspiration.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Present your "Field Intelligence" at the NSM. Help the team prioritize matching features and algorithm logic based on real-world employment urgency versus technical "nice-to-haves."
Sprint 6 Staggered Look-Ahead: Work with the PM to identify the key stakeholders needed for the validation of the Reporting and Analytics Module, which will be your primary focus next week.

Cards.Name: Tactical Blueprint and User Story
Cards.Description:
This week, you are the Bridge between the current build and the next logistical phase of the project.

Your mission is to translate the vision from the global roadmap into a detailed, actionable User Story for the AI Matching Module.

Sprint 4 is about removing every technical and logical blocker before we start coding the matching features in Sprint 5.

You are the team’s Unlocker.

We are shifting focus from internal data management to user experience, exploring how job seeker profiles for candidates with disabilities and job postings will be analyzed to facilitate optimal matches, including application submissions and status updates.

Success means defining a clear, actionable workflow that transforms user data into a ranked list of job opportunities for seekers, compatible candidates for employers, and a smooth application tracking process.

By the end of this week, the team must know exactly what to build for the next module, why, and how.

Every decision you document now regarding the Matching Experience ensures the developers and designers can execute the build with total focus and zero ambiguity.
ChecklistItems: Meeting Orchestration: Schedule a short Sprint 4 Status Meeting at the start of the week for team alignment. Schedule the Next Sprint Meeting (NSM) for the second half of the week.
Status Sync Recap: Publish a concise summary of the week’s setup tasks and any immediate blockers identified during the status meeting to the Team Chat.

Customer Validation: Interview the AI Customer to finalize the required matching criteria (e.g., Candidate Abilities vs. Job Tasks and Environmental Needs) and identify the specific algorithms for compatibility scoring and ranking.

The User Story Deep-Dive: Locate the existing AI Matching Module card in the “User Stories” Trello list and fill it out completely:
(1) Description Field: Write the formal story ("As a job seeker with disabilities... I want... so that..."), define the In-Scope/Out-of-Scope lists, and include a Data Table listing every Field Name (e.g., Abilities, Work Preferences, Physical/Cognitive Requirements, Job Characteristics, Application Status), Data Type, and required User Actions (e.g., Submit Application, Update Status).
(2) Acceptance Criteria Checklist: Populate the pre-made, empty checklist on the card with specific “Done” conditions relevant to the module’s core logic, accessibility considerations, technical requirements, and successful handling of application submissions and status updates.

Design Alignment: Sync with the UI/UX Designer to review the interface designs for job seeker and employer views. Ensure the final design, including the visual language, is saved in the Squad Room before the sprint ends.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Lead the planning session.

 (2) Facilitate T-shirt sizing (S/M/L) 
 (3) Confirm developer understanding of the matching logic 
 (4) Assign owners for Sprint 5
Sprint 5 Launch Sync: Publish a consolidated summary of the build goals and a clear list of prioritized action items in the Team Chat to formally trigger the start of the execution phase.

Cards.Name: AI Matching Logic Development
Cards.Description:
This week, the AI Matching Agent takes center stage.

You are no longer just an administrator of data; you are the AI Matching Specialist.

Your mission is to build the LLM-based logic that interprets job seeker profiles and job postings to generate compatibility scores.

By the end of this sprint, your module must be able to analyze input data, leverage the LLM to reason over abilities, preferences, and job requirements, and generate ranked lists of job opportunities for seekers and compatible candidates for employers.

You are responsible for ensuring that the AI Matching Agent foundation is reliable and provides the necessary endpoints for real-time matching updates.

Matching Logic Build: Conduct a technical deep-dive into the LLM-based reasoning logic relevant to compatibility scoring. Code the logic to analyze job seeker profiles against job postings, consulting with the AI Mentor to ensure the integration is secure and performant.

API Endpoint Development: Build the GET endpoints for retrieving ranked job opportunities for seekers and compatible candidates for employers, alongside the PATCH/PUT endpoints to handle job seekers submitting applications and employers updating application statuses.

Verify that your endpoints are serving the correct data structure for displaying ranked job opportunities to job seekers, compatible candidate lists to employers, and handling application submissions and status updates.

 Your mission is to build the Analytics Hub for the Reporting and Analytics Module. 
 
 You are no longer just capturing or managing data; you are responsible for making it meaningful. By the end of this sprint, your interface must be able to render high-density tables and dynamic charts that provide a clear narrative of the platform's impact on inclusive employment. 
 
 You are responsible for ensuring that complex datasets are presented with high glanceability and that the filtering logic is seamless, allowing stakeholders to extract the specific insights they need to drive the organization forward.
ChecklistItems: Sprint 6 Launch: Participate in the Sprint Meeting led by the PM. Review the User Story and Acceptance Criteria to ensure the reporting interface and data visualization logic match the business requirements.
Environment Setup: Create your Sprint 6 branch via the mentor buttons panel to begin the build.

Temporary Access Point: Add an "Analytics Hub" button to the index.html file "frontend-buttons" panel. This ensures the reporting interface is accessible to all team members via the "Live Project" link during development. As a reminder, the final professional Landing Page will be the focus of Sprint 7.

Analytics UI Implementation: Transform your reporting atoms into a dynamic interface. Implement the layouts for data visualization widgets (charts/graphs) and high-density tables as defined in the UI/UX designs.

Filtering & Parameter Logic: Code the client-side logic to handle dynamic report parameters. Ensure the interface correctly updates when users apply filters such as dates, specific categories, or job sectors.

API Integration (The Wiring): Connect your Analytics Hub to the Backend’s GET endpoints for aggregated datasets. Implement the logic to parse complex JSON structures and map them to your chart and table components.

Interaction & Feedback Handling: Build the logic for interactive data states. Ensure the UI provides clear feedback during data fetching (loading states) and handles empty result sets or filtering errors gracefully.
Peer Code Review: Share your code with the AI Mentor for a review session to ensure your data mapping logic is efficient and your chart components are performant.

Integration Test (Internal): Run a full end-to-end test of the reporting flow. Confirm that the interface correctly renders aggregated data and that all filters trigger the expected updates from the API.
Backend Handshake: Pair with the Backend Developer to verify that the aggregated data format served by their endpoints perfectly matches the expectations of your visualization components.

Next Module Requirements Sync: Meet with the PM once the Application Landing Page requirements are drafted to identify the specific visual assets, branding elements, and navigation links needed for the final phase.
Technical Readiness Audit: Prepare the environment for the PM’s audit by ensuring the structural "Atoms" for the Landing Page (hero sections, feature cards) are ready for the Sprint 7 build.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to plan the development of the Application Landing Page. This session serves as the primary planning phase for the final project sprint, though it can also be used to demonstrate the functional Analytics Hub.
Feature Shipping (PR & Merge): Push your final code, create a Pull Request (PR), and merge it into main via the mentor buttons panel to officially complete the Reporting and Analytics experience.

Cards.Name: Strategic Logic and Impact-ROI Modeling
Cards.Description:
This week, you are transitioning the project from a "functional application" to a "measurable socio-economic solution." 
 
 Your mission is to develop a comprehensive Logic Model that maps the causal link between our technological inputs and the tangible social and economic value we create. 
 
 You are the team's Value Architect. 
 
 Success means establishing a quantitative framework that proves how the platform optimizes resources. By the end of this week, you will define the "Value Equation" to serve as the core data for investor pitches. All finalized documentation must be saved under the Resources section in the Squad Room.
ChecklistItems: Logic Model Framework (Inputs & Outputs): Define the resources invested and map them to the platform’s technical deliverables (e.g., reporting tools, algorithms), ensuring the Strategic Logic Model Document is finalized and saved to the Resources folder.

mpact Analysis (Outcomes): Identify specific social and community outcomes, such as increased employment of job seekers with disabilities, improved job retention, and enhanced workplace accessibility, to provide a clear narrative of the platform's impact on inclusive employment.

Economic ROI Modeling (Business Stakeholders): Analyze financial benefits for participating businesses, including tax incentives, operational savings, and brand equity, and save the finalized ROI & Business Value Analysis to the Resources section.

Stakeholder Value Proposition: Quantify the benefits for employers, training providers, and community organizations, such as reduced recruitment barriers, lower turnover costs, and more efficient placement of job seekers with disabilities, to support long-term partnership sustainability.
The "Value Equation" Synthesis: Formulate a high-impact KPI (e.g., 3.5x ROI) to demonstrate efficiency and document the Executive Summary: The Value Equation in the Resources section.
Pitch Material Integration: Translate the Logic Model and ROI data into high-impact slides, ensuring the Updated Investor Pitch Deck is saved under Resources for upcoming presentations.
Stakeholder Validation: Meet with the AI Customer to verify that the ROI assumptions align with actual operational costs and budget categories.
Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Present the completed Logic Model and ROI data to the team to align the marketing narrative with the new quantitative data.

Cards.Name: Tactical Blueprint and Risk-Aware Planning
Cards.Description:
This week, you are managing the transition toward an enhanced user interaction platform through the Accessibility API Module.

Your mission is to define the requirements for two key areas: the Application Landing Page and the Voice Input Processing.

Sprint 6 is about resolving logical and technical dependencies before the final execution in Sprint 7.

You are the team’s Unlocker.

Success means establishing a clear structure for the landing page and a voice input processing that effectively captures user responses, ensuring accessibility for all users.

By the end of this week, the team must have the data specifications and user flows necessary to implement these features without technical ambiguity.
ChecklistItems: Meeting Orchestration: Schedule a short Sprint 6 Status Meeting at the start of the week for team alignment. Schedule the Next Sprint Meeting (NSM) for the second half of the week.
Status Sync Recap: Publish a concise summary of the week’s setup tasks and any immediate blockers identified during the status meeting to the Team Chat.
Customer Validation: Interview the AI Customer to finalize the primary messaging for the Voice Input Processing and define the specific accessibility features that must be highlighted.

The User Story (Voice Input Processing): Locate the dedicated card in the Trello “User Stories” list and fill it out. Draft a general narrative that defines the logic and criteria for processing voice recordings into structured text. The story should outline how the system captures audio input and ensures it is accurately converted for user profiles.

The User Story (User Profile Integration): Locate the dedicated card in the Trello “User Stories” list and fill it out. Draft a simple narrative that outlines the key sections needed for integrating voice responses into user profiles. Focus on defining the content structure and the necessary fields for seamless data incorporation.

Design Alignment: Sync with the UI/UX Designer to review the high-fidelity designs for the voice input interface. Ensure the visual branding and content hierarchy are finalized and saved in the Squad Room before the sprint ends.

Technical Requirements Sync: Meet with the Backend Developer to define the API Integration Path. Identify how the backend will process the voice recordings and log the structured text results within the system architecture.

Technical Readiness Audit: Verify the development environment. Confirm the backend has the schema updated for voice input logging and the frontend has prepared the structural containers for the Application Landing Page

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Lead the final planning session for the project build. 
 Agenda: 
 (1) Present the Voice Input Processing and Landing Page User Stories 
 (2) Facilitate T-shirt sizing (S/M/L) for the remaining tasks 
 (3) Confirm developer understanding of the voice data integration 
 (4) Assign owners for the final Sprint 7 build phase
Sprint 7 Launch Sync: Publish a consolidated summary of the final build goals and a list of prioritized action items in the Team Chat to formally trigger the start of the execution phase.

Cards.Name: The Public Face - Landing Page Design
Cards.Description:
This week, you move from internal operational tools to the public-facing storefront of the platform.

Your mission is to design the high-fidelity Application Landing Page that will serve as the gateway for job seekers with disabilities, employers, training providers, and community partners.

You are the Guardian of the Brand.

By the end of this week, you must provide the developers with a "Visual Source of Truth" that defines the platform's value proposition, feature highlights, and call-to-action flow. Your goal is to ensure the first impression of the platform is professional, trustworthy, and clearly communicates the mission of inclusive employment and accessibility for candidates with disabilities.
ChecklistItems: Customer Requirement Sync: Interview the AI Customer to finalize the primary messaging, value propositions, and the target audience priorities for the landing page.

PM Requirement Sync: Meet with the PM as soon as the Landing Page user story is drafted. Review the defined content hierarchy and acceptance criteria to ensure your visual strategy is perfectly aligned with the project requirements.

Landing Page Design (High-Fidelity): Create the comprehensive layout in Figma. 
 (1) Hero Section: Design the primary visual hook and headline area to grab immediate attention. 
 (2) Feature Highlights: Create the visual framework for demonstrating the platform's core modules (Profile, Posting, Matching). 
 (3) Impact Metrics: Design the section for displaying community impact data and social proof to build trust.

Call-to-Action (CTA) Design: Establish the visual hierarchy for buttons and links. Ensure the primary conversion paths (e.g., "Find Jobs," "Post a Job," "Learn More") are prominent, intuitive, and clearly guide job seekers with disabilities, employers, and partners through the platform.
Responsive Design Framework: Define how the landing page scales across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports to ensure a seamless experience for all visitors.

Technical Handshake: Sync with the Frontend Developer to review the landing page designs. 
 Synchronization: Confirm the feasibility of the proposed layouts and asset types. 
 Feasibility: Ensure the design components are optimized for fast loading and mobile performance.

Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to present the finalized Landing Page designs to the team. 
 Agenda: 
 (1) Present the Branding and Landing Page layouts 
 (2) Demonstrate the visitor journey and CTA flow 
 (3) Confirm design handoff for the Sprint 7 "Grand Finale" build

Cards.Name: Final Feature Integration
Cards.Description:
This is the final feature sprint. Your mission is to complete the technical core of the Accessibility API Module by implementing the voice input processing service, providing an enhanced interaction layer for users with accessibility needs. 
 
 Your focus is on ensuring the voice recognition logic is robust and the system architecture is fully operational for its primary functional debut. You are the System Anchor. 
 
 Success means a module with fully operational voice input processing that is integrated, tested, and merged. By the end of this week, the backend feature set must be 100% complete and documented.
ChecklistItems: Sprint 7 Launch: Participate in the Sprint Meeting led by the PM. Review the User Story and Acceptance Criteria to ensure you are 100% aligned on the logistics build goals.
Environment Setup: Create your Sprint 7 branch via the mentor buttons panel to begin the build.

Voice Processing Integration: Implement the logic for the voice input processing service. Create the endpoint to receive audio recordings and integrate the transcription engine to convert voice data into structured text using Google Speach-to-Text API.
Accessibility Logic: Define the backend processes for handling voice input. Ensure the system correctly logs the transcription results and integrates them into the user's employment profile.

Profile Data Integration: Update the Employment Profile APIs to incorporate the structured text from voice inputs. Ensure that the internal management flow can retrieve and display these updates seamlessly during the profile creation process.

Peer Code Review: Conduct a thorough review of the voice processing logic and Employment Profile integration with the AI Mentor. Focus on the accuracy of the transcription and the efficiency of the data logging before the final merge.

Final PR & Merge: Complete the final Pull Request for all Sprint 7 features. Conduct a rigorous code review and merge the branch into the main repository once all quality checks are passed to establish the definitive feature-complete codebase.
Project Handoff Documentation: Finalize the API Documentation and System Architecture Map, ensuring all finalized technical docs are saved under the Resources section in the Squad Room.
Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to demonstrate the voice input processing logic to the team and confirm the backend feature set is complete.

Cards.Name: Final Feature Integration
Cards.Description:
This is the final feature sprint. Your mission is to bring the platform to life by building the public-facing Landing Page and finalizing the frontend architecture.

You are the bridge between the project’s strategic value and the user’s experience. You are moving from internal administrative tools to a polished, professional storefront that communicates the mission of inclusive employment for job seekers with disabilities to the world.

Success means a pixel-perfect, responsive landing page that serves as the gateway for job seekers, employers, training providers, and community partners. By the end of this week, the frontend feature set must be 100% complete and documented.
ChecklistItems: Sprint 7 Launch: Participate in the Sprint Meeting led by the PM. Review the User Story and Acceptance Criteria to ensure you are 100% aligned on the build goals.
Environment Setup: Create your Sprint 7 branch via the mentor buttons panel to begin the build.

Landing Page Implementation: Build the high-fidelity, responsive Landing Page in React based on the finalized Figma designs. Ensure the Hero section, Feature Highlights, and Impact Metrics are visually consistent and optimized for all screen sizes. Once completed, replace the existing index.html file with this final implementation.

Peer Code Review: Conduct a thorough review of the Landing Page code with the AI Mentor. Focus on component reusability, CSS responsiveness, and alignment with the UI Documentation before proceeding to the final merge.

Final PR & Merge: Complete the final Pull Request for all Sprint 7 features. Conduct a thorough code review and merge the branch into the main repository once all quality checks are passed to establish the definitive feature-complete codebase.
Project Handoff Documentation: Finalize the Frontend Component Library and UI Documentation, ensuring all finalized technical docs are saved under the Resources section in the Squad Room.
Next Sprint Meeting (NSM): Participate in the NSM to demonstrate the live Landing Page to the team and confirm the frontend feature set is complete.

Cards.Name: The "Go-to-Market" Launch Campaign
Cards.Description:
This is the final push. Your mission is to lead a collaborative "Marketing Task Force" alongside the UI/UX Designer and the PM to launch the platform into the wild. 
 
 You are moving from the "building" phase to the "telling" phase. This sprint is entirely focused on creating the narrative and visual tools required to convince the world that this solution isn't just a project, it’s an essential community asset. Since the three of you are operating as a single unit, you will decide internally how to distribute the workload, but the goal remains the same: a high-impact, professional suite of marketing assets. 
 
 Success means having a cohesive brand presence across all platforms. By the end of this week, all campaign materials must be finalized and accessible in the Squad Room.
ChecklistItems: Marketing Strategy Sync: Convene with the PM and UI/UX Designer to finalize the core campaign message. Ensure the strategy effectively bridges technical features with the economic ROI to create a compelling "why now" for potential partners and donors.

Social Media Campaign: Design and draft a set of high-impact posts for LinkedIn and Instagram that showcase the platform in action, ensuring that the link to the finalized Social Media Content Calendar and Assets is saved under the Resources section.

Final Pitch Deck: Refine the presentation for online delivery, focusing on the story of job seeking with disability and the data-driven impact, and save the link to the Online Presentation Deck under the Resources section in the Squad Room.

YouTube Promotional Video: Develop the script and storyboard for a professional explainer movie that highlights the user journey and the platform's value, ensuring the link to the YouTube Script and Storyboard is saved under the Resources section.

Impact Storytelling (Success Story): Transform a customer interview or use case into a narrative-driven success story that humanizes the platform's benefits, ensuring the link to this Impact Case Study is saved under the Resources section.

Cross-Functional Asset Audit: Conduct a final review of all marketing materials as a team to ensure that the messaging from the PM, the visuals from UI/UX, and the strategy from BizDev are perfectly aligned for a unified brand voice.

Final Resource Consolidation: Verify that the links for every piece of the "Go-to-Market" puzzle are organized, labeled, and securely listed under the Resources section of the Squad Room for the project's official conclusion.

[ ] Social Media Collaboration: Review and contribute to the drafting of high-impact social media posts. Ensure the link to the finalized Social Media Content Calendar and Assets is saved under the Resources section.

[ ] Pitch Deck Review: Collaborate on the Online Presentation Deck, focusing on the accuracy of the functional features and impact metrics. Ensure the link to the final deck is saved under the Resources section in the Squad Room.

ChecklistItems: [ ] Marketing Strategy Sync: Participate in the strategy session led by the BizDev lead. Help the team translate the platform's value proposition into a visual narrative that resonates with the target audience.

[ ] Social Media Campaign: Collaborate on the design and copy for high-impact social media posts. Ensure the link to the finalized Social Media Content Calendar and Assets is saved under the Resources section.

[ ] Final Pitch Deck: Support the refinement of the Online Presentation Deck. Focus on ensuring the layout is professional and effectively showcases the project's impact. Save the link to the final deck under the Resources section in the Squad Room.

Cards.Name: Stabilization & Final Polish
Cards.Description:
Your mission is to switch from feature building to a collaborative "Bug Hunting" phase alongside the entire team to ensure the platform is production-ready. 
 
 You are moving from the "feature" phase to the "quality" phase. This sprint is entirely focused on identifying glitches and ensuring the system logic is flawless before the final handoff. Since everyone is doing QA, you will monitor the Meeting Room for every "Bugs case" logged and address them in your dedicated branch. 
 
 Success means a robust, error-free backend that has been fully merged into the main repository.
ChecklistItems: [ ] Sprint 8 Launch: Participate in the final Sprint Planning meeting led by the PM. Review the QA process and the "Bugs" sprint objectives to ensure alignment on the stabilization goals.
[ ] Environment Setup: Create your dedicated "Bugs" branch via the mentor buttons panel. This will be the single point of truth for all code fixes during this sprint.

[ ] Team-Wide QA & Bug Discovery: Join the entire team in a comprehensive QA cycle. Monitor the Meeting Room for any new Bugs case logged by the PM, BizDev, or designers, and verify each against the backend logic.
[ ] Bug Resolution: Systematically address every Bugs case related to the backend. Focus on API stability, data validation, and logic errors to ensure the system is robust.
[ ] Peer Bug Review: Conduct a thorough review of your fixes with the AI Mentor. Ensure that bug resolutions are optimized and do not introduce new regressions into the established feature set.

[ ] Final PR & Merge: Complete the final Pull Request for the "Bugs" branch. Conduct a final rigorous code review and merge the stabilized code into the main repository to establish the project's definitive version.
[ ] Project Summary Meeting: Participate in the final meeting led by the PM to review the project's overall journey, celebrate the successful build, and confirm the platform is stable.

Cards.Name: Stabilization & Final Polish

Since everyone is doing QA, you will monitor the Meeting Room for every "Bugs case" logged and address them in your dedicated branch. 
 
 Success means a pixel-perfect, error-free frontend that has been fully merged into the main repository.

[ ] Environment Setup: Create your dedicated "Bugs" branch via the mentor buttons panel. This will be your primary workspace for all UI and logic fixes this week.

[ ] Team-Wide QA & Bug Discovery: Join the entire team in a comprehensive QA cycle. Monitor the Meeting Room for any new Bugs case logged by the PM, BizDev, or designers, and verify each against the frontend implementation.
[ ] Bug Resolution: Systematically address every Bugs case related to the frontend. Focus on visual glitches, responsive design breaks, and interaction errors to ensure a smooth user journey.

[ ] Project Summary Meeting: Participate in the final meeting led by the PM to review the project's overall journey, celebrate the successful build, and confirm the platform is ready for launch.

Cards.Name: Stabilization & Project Summary
Cards.Description:
Your mission is to switch from the marketing launch to a collaborative "Bug Hunting" phase alongside the entire team to ensure the platform is production-ready. 
 
 You are moving from the "telling" phase to the "quality and closure" phase. This sprint is entirely focused on identifying glitches and ensuring the business logic and user experience are flawless before the final handoff. Since everyone is doing QA, you will monitor the platform as a user and log every "Bugs case" in the Meeting room. 
 
 Success means a stable platform and a finalized set of marketing materials that perfectly reflect the project's final state for the Project Summary meeting.
ChecklistItems: [ ] Sprint 8 Launch: Participate in the final Sprint Planning meeting led by the PM. Review the QA process and understand how to log and track issues during this stabilization week.

[ ] Team-Wide QA & Logging: Dive into the platform from a "customer" perspective. Conduct thorough testing of the user journeys and log every Bugs case you discover in the Meeting room for the developers to address.

[ ] Marketing Asset Alignment: Review the Pitch Deck, Social Media posts, and YouTube Video created in Sprint 7. Ensure they reflect the final version of the product and update any links or screenshots if necessary.

[ ] Project Summary Preparation: Prepare your highlights and "lessons learned" for the final Project Summary meeting. Be ready to share the business impact and the growth potential of the project with the team.
[ ] Final Resource Audit: Conduct a final check of the Resources folder to ensure all marketing links and business documentation are organized and accessible for the final project closure.

Cards.Name: Project Summary & Quality Assurance
Cards.Description:
This is the final stabilization push. Your mission is to coordinate the collaborative "Bug Hunting" phase alongside the entire team while preparing the final project closure. 
 
 You are moving from the "oversight" phase to the "quality and closure" phase. This sprint is entirely focused on identifying every glitch and facilitating the final Project Summary. Since everyone is doing QA, you will lead by example, logging every "Bugs case" in the Meeting room and ensuring the developers are unblocked to resolve them. 
 
 Success means a stable platform and a team that is fully aligned on the project's achievements during the final summary meeting.
ChecklistItems: [ ] Sprint 8 Launch: Lead the final Sprint Planning meeting. Define the QA protocols and set the expectations for the "Bugs case" logging process to ensure the team is synced for the final week.

[ ] Team-Wide QA & Logging: Dive into the platform as a user. Conduct thorough end-to-end testing and log every Bugs case discovered in the Meeting room, ensuring each report is clear and actionable for the developers.

[ ] Trello Bug Management: Manage the Bugs case queue exclusively via Trello (PM access only). Assign the priority and severity for each logged issue to ensure the developers are focused on the most critical stability tasks first.

[ ] Developer Coordination: Monitor the progress of the dedicated "Bugs" branches. Ensure that both the Backend and Frontend developers complete their final PR and merge to the main repository by the end of the sprint.

[ ] Project Summary Meeting Organization: Schedule and prepare the agenda for the final Project Summary meeting. Ensure all team members are invited to share their highlights, challenges, and final results.

[ ] Final Project Wrap-up: Conduct a final audit of the Resources folder in the Squad Room. Verify that all sprint deliverables, marketing assets, and technical docs (links) are correctly organized for the final handoff.
[ ] Project Summary Presentation: Lead the final team session. Celebrate the successful build, review the "Bugs case" resolution metrics, and officially conclude the project.

Cards.Name: Stabilization & Project Summary

 You are moving from the "creation" phase to the "quality" phase. This sprint is entirely focused on identifying visual and interaction glitches before the final handoff. Since everyone is doing QA, you will monitor the platform and log every "Bugs case" in the Meeting room. 
 
 Success means a pixel-perfect, stable platform and a finalized brand presence for the Project Summary meeting.
ChecklistItems: [ ] Sprint 8 Launch: Participate in the final Sprint Planning meeting led by the PM. Review the QA protocols and understand the workflow for logging and tracking UI/UX issues.

[ ] Team-Wide QA & Logging: Dive into the platform as a user. Conduct thorough "visual QA" and log every Bugs case you discover in the Meeting room, focusing on spacing, alignment, and interaction flows.

[ ] Visual Integrity Check: Monitor the bug fixes being implemented by developers. Ensure that no fix accidentally breaks the official color palette, logo placement, or typography standards established for the project.

[ ] Marketing Asset Alignment: Review the Pitch Deck, Social Media graphics, and YouTube Video. Ensure they reflect the final state of the platform and replace any outdated screenshots if bug fixes have changed the UI.
[ ] Project Summary Preparation: Prepare your highlights and "design evolution" notes for the final Project Summary meeting. Be ready to showcase how the UI/UX helped achieve the project goals.
[ ] Final Resource Audit: Conduct a final check of the Resources folder to ensure all design links, brand assets, and marketing visuals are organized and accessible for the final project closure.
